full_response:
We work with the Schwarzschild metric (signature − + + +), for which the nonzero components are  
  g_tt = − (1 − 2GM/r)\, , g_rr = (1 − 2GM/r)⁻¹\,,  g_θθ = r²\,,  g_φφ = r² sin²θ.  

Our curve is  
  r(λ) = r₀,  θ(λ) = θ₀,  φ(λ) = φ₀,  
and its tangent (in the coordinate basis with λ = t) is  
  U^μ = (dt/dλ, dr/dλ, dθ/dλ, dφ/dλ) = (1, 0, 0, 0)\,.  

(a) Compute the norm  
  U·U = g_{μν}\,U^μ U^ν  
       = g_tt (U^t)² + g_rr (U^r)² + …  
       = g_tt·1²  
       = −\Bigl(1 − \frac{2GM}{r₀}\Bigr)\,.  

Since we denote R_S = 2GM, this is  
  U·U = − (1 − R_S/r₀)\,.  

For r₀ > R_S we have 1 − R_S/r₀ > 0, so U·U < 0.  

Thus U is timelike (but not unit–normalized).  

Final Answer  
\boxed{\,U\cdot U \;=\; -\bigl(1-\tfrac{2GM}{r_0}\bigr)\,,\quad\text{which is negative for }r_0>2GM\;\Longrightarrow\;U\text{ is timelike.}}  

Confidence: 95%
